name: Mandarin Pancakes (Doilies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place flour in a mixing bowl.
2. Gradually stir in boiling water, mixing with a wooden spoon.
3. When it is cool enough to handle, knead with your hands until it is smooth and elastic, adding a little more flour if necessary.
4. Cover dough with a damp dish towel and let rest for 30 minutes.
5. Roll dough out on a lightly floured surface to a 1/4 inch thickness.
6. Use a 3-inch round cookie cutter to cut the dough, rerolling it until all of the dough has been used.
7. Smear sesame oil over one side of each 3-inch circle of dough.
8. Put two circles together with the sesame-oiled sides touching.
9. Roll out the dough circles until they are about 6 inches in diameter, turning as you roll so they keep a round shape.
10. Cover the pancakes with a damp towel until ready to cook.
11. Use an ungreased heavy skillet over low heat and roast one side of the pancake until it bubbles slightly (about 1 minute). Do not brown.
12. Turn over and roast the other side. Remove from the skillet.
13. When cool enough to handle, pull the two pancakes apart.
14. Wrap the pancakes in foil and refrigerate.
15. To Reheat: Fold each pancake into quarters and place in a steamer. Steam for 5 minutes. Alternatively, fold the pancakes and place on top of cooked rice; the heat and steam from the rice will reheat them.
